Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: It's amazing to think about the fervor that surrounded

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: broke back mountain when it was released 11 years ago.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: Heath, Ang, and I were at the center of a storm that year,

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: fielding the praise and controversy that came from telling a love story about two men.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: Standing here tonight, I can't help but think about Heath

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: and how much he would have loved to witness all of this incredible change

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: that has been affected in the LGBT community in the intervening years.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: He would be proud to know that he had somehow played a small part in all of it.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: I know that I am.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: And even though broke back was very much a movie of its moment in history,

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: Ang made sure that it would stand the test of time.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: He kept it simple and quiet with respect for the characters

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: and their corner of the world.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: He knew that to tell a big story, you have to keep it small.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: Ang, like any master director, though there aren't many,

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: is an extraordinary teacher.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: And while some authors lead their troops with flash and bang working

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: with Ang is a master class in silence.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: We don't always need to speak in order to communicate.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: And it's a lesson that rings in my ears often.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: I remember the day we shot our big love scene.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: When we finished the scene, both Heath and I, we got up,

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: and we looked over to see a huge smile on Ang's face.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: And I was surprised because although you might not know it,

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: Ang has a really great sense of humor.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: He rarely smiled on set.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: But this was a shit-eating grin.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: And so naturally I walked up to Ang,

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: and I was excited to see any sort of emotion across his face.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: And eagerly, I asked, I was like, how was that?

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: How was that?

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: You know, you like the scene?

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: And he just was nodding in a really awkward way.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: And he just said, so gay.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: That was the first of many times that he'd say that.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: But what makes him so unique

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: and so deserving of our highest honor

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: is that Ang realizes that each film is elementally different.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: And instead of choosing scripts that are in his wheelhouse

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: or bending narratives to meet his style,

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: he meets his stories where they are.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: And he gives them what they need.
